---
title: "The pipeline in one city"
subtitle: "From commuting and education to complexity and sectors in Mexico City"
author: "Ollin D. Langle-Chimal"
date: today
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    html-math-method: katex
jupyter: python3
---

## Setup

Mexico City makes the whole construction legible at one glance. We rebuild its location
complexity from the commuting panel, map it over the metro, read the dominant sector in
each work cell, and project complexity onto sectors.

```{python}
import os, sys
os.chdir("..")
sys.path.insert(0, "src")

from eci import load_panel, location_eci
from data import load_mx_workers
from sectors import worker_weighted_sector_eci, dominant_sector
from figures import style, eci_map, dominant_sector_map, sector_bar

style()

eci = location_eci(load_panel("MX"))
eci["geomid"] = eci["geomid"].astype(str)
mx = eci[eci["city"] == "mexico_city"]
workers = load_mx_workers()
```

## Complexity across Mexico City

```{python}
eci_map(eci, "mexico_city", "pipeline_eci_map")
```

![ECI over Mexico City](../figs/pipeline_eci_map.png)

Complexity peaks along the Reforma and Polanco corridor and in the western business
districts, and falls off toward the eastern and northern periphery.

## Dominant sector

```{python}
dominant_sector_map(dominant_sector(workers), set(mx["geomid"]),
                    "pipeline_dominant_sector", "Dominant sector, Mexico City")
```

![Dominant sector](../figs/pipeline_dominant_sector.png)

Retail blankets the residential periphery, manufacturing follows the industrial
corridors, and the complex core carries the service and administrative sectors.

## Complexity by sector

```{python}
ss = worker_weighted_sector_eci(workers, mx)
sector_bar(ss, "pipeline_sector_complexity")
ss.round(3)
```

![Mean sectoral complexity](../figs/pipeline_sector_complexity.png)

Projecting complexity onto sectors, weighting each work cell by the workers a sector
employs there, ranks corporate, professional services and construction at the top and
retail, manufacturing and education at the bottom.
